message:
feat(tui): add frame timing telemetry, API key login flow, and model selector scope fallback

Three complementary TUI improvements:

1. Frame Timing Telemetry (PERF-3):
   - Add FrameTimingStats with microsecond-precision rolling-window
     tracking for view(), content build, viewport sync, and update()
   - Gated behind PI_PERF_TELEMETRY=1 env variable (zero overhead when
     disabled) to avoid impacting normal usage
   - Track p50/p95/p99 percentiles over a 60-frame window with
     budget-exceeded counting against the 16.67ms frame budget
   - Emit periodic debug-level telemetry and include summary in /info
   - Instrument refresh_conversation_viewport with viewport sync timing

2. API Key Login Flow:
   - Add normalize_api_key_input() for trimming and whitespace rejection,
     normalize_auth_provider_input() for canonical provider mapping, and
     api_key_login_prompt() for guided key entry for OpenAI and Google
   - Wire /login command to detect providers with built-in key login
     (OpenAI, Google) and enter a sensitive single-line input mode
     instead of requiring OAuth
   - Add PendingLoginKind enum (ApiKey vs OAuth) to distinguish
     credential types, with differentiated success messages
   - Use canonical_provider_id for model lookup during OAuth config
     resolution so aliases like "gemini" resolve correctly
   - Update /logout to use generic "stored credentials" terminology

3. Model Selector Scope Fallback:
   - When model scope is configured but resolves to zero matches, fall
     back to cycling all available models instead of showing "No models
     in scope" dead end
   - Add explicit "No scoped models matched; cycling all available
     models" status message so users understand the fallback
   - /scoped-models now reports "0 matched; cycling will use all
     available models" when no patterns match

Also extends auth.rs to attempt canonical_provider_id fallback when
resolving stored API keys, so keys stored under an alias still resolve.

Includes comprehensive tests for all three features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
